channel.title: Windows on LinuxRepos
channel.description: Recent content in Windows on LinuxRepos

item.title: Im Sorry Netlify
item.description: I’ve used Netlify for years on my website and it’s a fantastic service! As I’ve grown it’s becoming unaffordable. I submitted a support request and this is what happened…

item.title: Fix Windows Network and Sharing
item.description: Fixing problems with windows file sharing using the following methods.

item.title: Theming Windows With Rainmeter
item.description: Rainmeter has some amazing themes, but this shows you methods to get a TRULY different look!

item.title: How to Sync Local Files
item.description: A guide over how to set up Syncthing for local sync only.

item.title: Replace Windows Search
item.description: Everything Search is faster, more efficient, and a better Windows Search. This guide shows you how to setup everything and a toolbar for quick access.

item.title: Dism++
item.description: Dism++ enables users to modify Windows images before installing them, as well as providing tools to address and repair common issues like missing or corrupted files.

item.title: Ext4 in Windows
item.description: Pulling in EXT4 drives is easy now with Windows WSL2. There are a few tricks that you need to know about though!

item.title: Why Open Source?
item.description: Why is open source done? The developer gives up all his code and potential for profit from the code.

item.title: Thumbnails in Windows
item.description: The thumbnail system in Windows has 4 major configuration areas dating back to 2000. From thumbs.db files, checkboxes, group policy, and registry edits, the Windows thumbnail system is, like everything in Windows, old and layered.

item.title: Block Razer Bloatware
item.description: This script is made due to the frustration of the bloatware that gets install for all Razer devices.

item.title: Virtual Machine Formats
item.description: This Guide goes over transferring and what each type of virtual machine is used for.

item.title: Acronis Cyber Protect Home Office
item.description: Acronis True Image has undergone an expansion and rename in the past couple years to Acronis Cyber Protect Home Office. This is a quick breakdown of their new offering.

item.title: Cleanup Docker
item.description: If you are running docker you will accumulate a lot of unused images and volumes as you update running containers. This makes maintenance a necessity.

item.title: Install Windows Update Powershell
item.description: Did you know you can install updates with using PowerShell? This is the Guide for doing that!

item.title: Screensavers
item.description: Screensavers might be the past, but they are still one of the coolest to do on your PC.

item.title: Docker Guide
item.description: This is a full walk-through and guide for Docker.

item.title: Windows Explorer Stealing CPU
item.description: Windows 11 has a bug effecting some systems. Out of my 3 Windows 11 systems, one of them had this happen.

item.title: Bypass Paywalls
item.description: Tired of ads, paywalls, and other garbage in your web browsing experience? These add-ons will improve your experience!

item.title: Twitter Archive Offline
item.description: Leaving Twitter? Why not download all your tweets from archive and store it yourself before deleting your account!

item.title: Docker Macos
item.description: Don’t have a Mac and need MacOS? No problem, run it in a docker container.

item.title: One Tool for Everything
item.description: A massive update has arrived for the Windows Utility! Check out all the new features.

item.title: Best Browser Extensions
item.description: These extensions make life easier when using a browser.

item.title: Why We Don't Browse the Internet Anymore
item.description: The problem with the modern internet is ads, sponsored posts, and pay walls. Enter RSS feeds as a solution.

item.title: Fix Corrupted Windows Install
item.description: Using SFC and DISM is often done in haste and incorrectly. If you need to fix a corrupted install, then a special DISM command MUST BE issued.

item.title: Shell The Essential App
item.description: Context menus have become worse in Windows 11 and weren’t good in Windows 10. This application will fix it for both modern Windows operating systems.

item.title: Windows 22H2 has Problems
item.description: I’ve had a ton of bug reports from 22H2. DO NOT UPDATE to this first Windows 11 update.

item.title: Neovim Is Better
item.description: I’ve never experienced a program with such power and potential than Neovim. It does so much and we are at the beginning as it isn’t even to a 1.0 release yet. The best part is the program works on Windows, MacOS, and Linux.

item.title: Urbackup
item.description: Want a Universal Backup that works on EVERY operating system and is free + open source? Urbackup is the software you are looking for!

item.title: Reagentc Windows Recovery Partition
item.description: Don’t know what ReAgentC is or how to use it? This is your guide.

item.title: Pretty Powershell
item.description: PowerShell is UGLY and cumbersome by default. This Guide fixes that!

item.title: Buying Cheap Ebay Graphics Cards
item.description: With GPU Market prices plummeting and about to go even further down… I picked up a used GPU from eBay.

item.title: Setting Up the Ultimate Windows 11
item.description: This guide shows you how to get the best performing Windows in the lightest package possible.

item.title: Why the Command Line is Overpowered
item.description: When I say “Overpowered”, this means the command line interfaces can do things that are otherwise impossible.

item.title: Pros Use Bleachbit
item.description: I still see far too many comments with people that still use CCLeaner. Bleachbit is a FAR superior product and it’s also free and open source. It won’t try to upsell you a bunch of garbage and has a great track record, unlike CCleaner which was hacked and literally installed malware on thousands of PCs.

item.title: How I Setup My Website
item.description: This shows you have I automate my workflows and setup my website for maximum productivity and search

item.title: NTLite Guide
item.description: This Guide shows you the easiest way to configure Windows and make an ISO with NTLite.

item.title: Be a Better Programmer
item.description: Many people want to be a programmer, but often find themselves flailing around in the dark. These are the things I’ve done to help aid myself in coding more, learning new things, and just becoming a better programmer.

item.title: ClickPaste the Best Program You Never Heard Of
item.description: This program is free and open source, while being absolutely a life saver. I use it for VNC sessions and when dealing with VMs through my host computer.

item.title: Setting up Windows inside Linux
item.description: Running Windows inside of Linux is essential for those that are trying to convert to Linux. There will always be a transition period and need to fallback to Windows. It is also nice to have for many Windows only programs that you may need while inside Linux.

item.title: You are using GitHub WRONG!
item.description: GitHub is an incredible powerful tool, but using it in command line can be frustrating. So instead of doing something dumb like using SSH agent workarounds or Token exporting shenanigans to make git work in command line. We are going to use an amazing utility called gh to authenticate.

item.title: Should you switch from Windows 10 to Windows 11
item.description: The question isn’t if you should switch, but if you CAN switch. Windows 11 does a lot of things right as most of it’s systems are identical to 10. However, there are some drawbacks which we need to dive into.

item.title: Laptop Power Management
item.description: Power management is a critical thing to understand in laptops. It’s possible to buy an expensive laptop and end up not using it because of bad power management practices.

item.title: Is Windows Bad
item.description: It’s what we know. Windows is a tool and has evolved over the years while dominating the PC Market since the 90s. So it begs the question, is it bad?

item.title: Disable Win Defender
item.description: Windows Defender can be a massive drain on system performance and often can have a lot of false positives. While I recommend Anti-virus for most, I don’t like having it enabled on my systems that aren’t used for web browsing.

item.title: Save HOURS of work with Regex
item.description: RegEx is a formidable tool that many just don’t understand, but can save literally thousands of hours of work.

item.title: Shrink VMWare VMDK File
item.description: This goes over reducing the size of a VMDK file using open-vm-tools and is used often in VMWare environments.

item.title: You are NOT safe in Windows
item.description: Thank you to all the Microsoft Windows users from all the Technicians that work on this operating system. Without your continued use, we wouldn’t have jobs.

item.title: Is Antivirus Worthless?
item.description: To many advanced users, Antivirus IS worthless. That is because it uses system resources and provides more annoyances than benefits. However, for many users it is required as they will not know what is a virus or not.

item.title: Future Internet
item.description: Renewing website domains, paying for web hosting, and having them expire are all going to be a thing of the past. Using IPFS (Inter-planetary File System) is a way to store websites decentralized and without any money. How fast or how slow it will be, is determined by how popular it is with IPFS nodes. This guide shows how to host a website, owning a web domain forever, and redirecting these domains to traditional website hosts.

item.title: The Ultimate Windows Utility
item.description: This is a tool that has evolved over several years and achieves much more than just debloating a Windows install. I have expanded it to install the programs you select, which is a bigger selection than ninite. The classic debloat is there, but also includes a tweaks section with quality of life features. To round out everything, I have included my recommended settings for Windows update that I use in a variety of businesses. Misuse of this utility can break your install so please be careful and I do not provide any help or assistance should this happen!

item.title: My Obsidian Setup
item.description: Notes are vital to our productivity but often people either take too many notes or none at all. I sought to fix this by using a simplistic approach in the Obsidian app. I’ve tried notion and a variety of other offerings that always fell flat. Let me show you how I set this app for maximum productivity.

item.title: Robocopy - Copying files the right way in Windows
item.description: This guide shows how to copy files in windows when dealing with large volumes of files and very long file paths past 255 characters.
